children.text: the term “Assistant Administrator” means the Assistant Administrator of the Office of Women’s Business Ownership established under subsection (g);
the term “private nonprofit organization” means an entity that is described in and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of such title;section 501(c) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: that is not less than 51 percent owned by 1 or more women; and

children.label: B
children.text: the management and daily business operations of which are controlled by 1 or more women; and

children.label: A
children.text: a women’s business center; or

children.label: i
children.text: that reach a distinct population that would otherwise not be served;

children.label: ii
children.text: whose services are targeted to women; and

children.label: iii
children.text: whose scope, function, and activities are similar to those of the primary women’s business center or centers in conjunction with which it was established.

financial assistance, including training and counseling in how to apply for and secure business credit and investment capital, preparing and presenting financial statements, and managing cash flow and other financial operations of a business concern;
management assistance, including training and counseling in how to plan, organize, staff, direct, and control each major activity and function of a small business concern; and

marketing assistance, including training and counseling in identifying and segmenting domestic and international market opportunities, preparing and executing marketing plans, developing pricing strategies, locating contract opportunities, negotiating contracts, and utilizing varying public relations and advertising techniques.

children.label: A
children.text: in the first and second years, 1 non-Federal dollar for each 2 Federal dollars; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the third, fourth, and fifth years, 1 non-Federal dollar for each Federal dollar.

children.heading: Form of non-Federal contributions
children.label: 2 Form of non-Federal contributions
children.text: Not more than one-half of the non-Federal sector matching assistance may be in the form of in-kind contributions that are budget line items only, including office equipment and office space.

children.heading: Form of Federal contributions
children.label: 3 Form of Federal contributions
children.text: The financial assistance authorized pursuant to this section may be made by grant, contract, or cooperative agreement and may contain such provision, as necessary, to provide for payments in lump sum or installments, and in advance or by way of reimbursement. The Administration may disburse up to 25 percent of each year’s Federal share awarded to a recipient organization after notice of the award has been issued and before the non-Federal sector matching funds are obtained.

children.heading: Failure to obtain non-Federal funding
children.label: 4 Failure to obtain non-Federal funding
children.text: If any recipient of assistance fails to obtain the required non-Federal contribution during any project, it shall not be eligible thereafter for advance disbursements pursuant to paragraph (3) during the remainder of that project, or for any other project for which it is or may be funded by the Administration, and prior to approving assistance to such organization for any other projects, the Administration shall specifically determine whether the Administration believes that the recipient will be able to obtain the requisite non-Federal funding and enter a written finding setting forth the reasons for making such determination.

children.heading: Contract authority
children.label: d Contract authority
children.text: A women’s business center may enter into a contract with a Federal department or agency to provide specific assistance to women and other underserved small business concerns. Performance of such contract should not hinder the women’s business centers in carrying out the terms of the grant received by the women’s business centers from the Administration.

children.heading: Submission of 5-year plan
children.label: e Submission of 5-year plan
children.text: Each applicant organization initially shall submit a 5-year plan to the Administration on proposed fundraising and training activities, and a recipient organization may receive financial assistance under this program for a maximum of 5 years per women’s business center site.
the experience of the applicant in conducting programs or ongoing efforts designed to impart or upgrade the business skills of women business owners or potential owners;
the present ability of the applicant to commence a project within a minimum amount of time;
the ability of the applicant to provide training and services to a representative number of women who are both socially and economically disadvantaged; and
the location for the women’s business center site proposed by the applicant.

children.heading: Office of Women’s Business Ownership
children.label: g Office of Women’s Business Ownership

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established within the Administration an Office of Women’s Business Ownership, which shall be responsible for the administration of the Administration’s programs for the development of women’s business enterprises (as defined in ). The Office of Women’s Business Ownership shall be administered by an Assistant Administrator, who shall be appointed by the Administrator.section 7108 of this title

children.heading: Assistant Administrator of the Office of Women’s Business Ownership
children.label: 2 Assistant Administrator of the Office of Women’s Business Ownership

children.heading: Qualification
children.label: A Qualification
children.text: The position of Assistant Administrator shall be a Senior Executive Service position under . The Assistant Administrator shall serve as a noncareer appointee (as defined in section 3132(a)(7) of that title).section 3132(a)(2) of title 5

children.label: I
children.text: starting and operating a small business;

children.label: II
children.text: development of management and technical skills;

children.label: III
children.text: seeking Federal procurement opportunities; and

children.label: IV
children.text: increasing the opportunity for access to capital.

children.label: I
children.text: administer and manage the Women’s Business Center program;

children.label: II
children.text: recommend the annual administrative and program budgets for the Office of Women’s Business Ownership (including the budget for the Women’s Business Center program);

children.label: III
children.text: establish appropriate funding levels therefore ;1So in original. Probably should be “therefor”.1

children.label: IV
children.text: review the annual budgets submitted by each applicant for the Women’s Business Center program;

children.label: V
children.text: select applicants to participate in the program under this section;

children.label: VI
children.text: implement this section;

children.label: VII
children.text: maintain a clearinghouse to provide for the dissemination and exchange of information between women’s business centers;

children.label: VIII
children.text: serve as the vice chairperson of the Interagency Committee on Women’s Business Enterprise;

children.label: IX
children.text: serve as liaison for the National Women’s Business Council; and

children.label: X
children.text: advise the Administrator on appointments to the Women’s Business Council.

children.heading: Consultation requirements
children.label: C Consultation requirements
children.text: In carrying out the responsibilities and duties described in this paragraph, the Assistant Administrator shall confer with and seek the advice of the Administration officials in areas served by the women’s business centers.

children.label: i
children.text: an itemized cost breakdown of actual expenditures for costs incurred during the preceding year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: documentation regarding the amount of matching assistance from non-Federal sources obtained and expended by the center during the preceding year in order to meet the requirements of subsection (c) and, with respect to any in-kind contributions described in subsection (c)(2) that were used to satisfy the requirements of subsection (c), verification of the existence and valuation of those contributions; and

children.label: B
children.text: analyze the results of each such examination and, based on that analysis, make a determination regarding the programmatic and financial viability of each women’s business center.

children.label: A
children.text: shall consider the results of the most recent examination of the center under paragraph (1); and

children.label: i
children.text: the center has failed to provide any information required to be provided under clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (1)(A), or the information provided by the center is inadequate; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the center has failed to provide any information required to be provided by the center for purposes of the report of the Administration under subsection (j), or the information provided by the center is inadequate.

children.heading: Contract authority
children.label: i Contract authority
children.text: The authority of the Administrator to enter into contracts shall be in effect for each fiscal year only to the extent and in the amounts as are provided in advance in appropriations Acts. After the Administrator has entered into a contract, either as a grant or a cooperative agreement, with any applicant under this section, it shall not suspend, terminate, or fail to renew or extend any such contract unless the Administrator provides the applicant with written notification setting forth the reasons therefore and affords the applicant an opportunity for a hearing, appeal, or other administrative proceeding under chapter 5 of title 5.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administration shall prepare and submit to the Committees on Small Business of the House of Representatives and the Senate a report on the effectiveness of all projects conducted under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of individuals receiving assistance;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of startup business concerns formed;

children.label: C
children.text: the gross receipts of assisted concerns;

children.label: D
children.text: the employment increases or decreases of assisted concerns;

children.label: E
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, increases or decreases in profits of assisted concerns; and

children.label: F
children.text: the most recent analysis, as required under subsection (h)(1)(B), and the subsequent determination made by the Administration under that subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: $12,000,000 for fiscal year 2000;

children.label: B
children.text: $12,800,000 for fiscal year 2001;

children.label: C
children.text: $13,700,000 for fiscal year 2002; and

children.label: D
children.text: $14,500,000 for fiscal year 2003.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), amounts made available under this subsection for fiscal year 1999, and each fiscal year thereafter, may only be used for grant awards and may not be used for costs incurred by the Administration in connection with the management and administration of the program under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: For fiscal year 2000, 2 percent.

children.label: ii
children.text: For fiscal year 2001, 1.9 percent.

children.label: iii
children.text: For fiscal year 2002, 1.9 percent.

children.label: iv
children.text: For fiscal year 2003, 1.6 percent.

children.heading: Expedited acquisition
children.label: 3 Expedited acquisition
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Administrator, acting through the Assistant Administrator, may use such expedited acquisition methods as the Administrator determines to be appropriate to carry out this section, except that the Administrator shall ensure that all small business sources are provided a reasonable opportunity to submit proposals.

children.heading: Reservation of funds for sustainability pilot program
children.label: 4 Reservation of funds for sustainability pilot program
i
children.text: For fiscal year 2000, 17 percent.

children.label: ii
children.text: For fiscal year 2001, 18.8 percent.

children.label: iii
children.text: For fiscal year 2002, 30.2 percent.

children.label: iv
children.text: For fiscal year 2003, 30.2 percent.

children.heading: Use of unawarded funds for sustainability pilot program grants
children.label: B Use of unawarded funds for sustainability pilot program grants
children.text: If the amount reserved under subparagraph (A) for any fiscal year is not fully awarded to private nonprofit organizations described in subsection ()(1)(B), the Administration is authorized to use the unawarded amount to fund additional women’s business center sites or to increase funding of existing women’s business center sites under subsection (b).l

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 110–28, title VIII, § 8305(b)121 Stat. 210May 25, 2007
children.label: l Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 110–28, title VIII, § 8305(b)121 Stat. 210May 25, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A nonprofit organization described in paragraph (2) shall be eligible to receive, subject to paragraph (3), a 3-year grant under this subsection.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: A nonprofit organization described in this paragraph is a nonprofit organization that has received funding under subsection (b) or ().l

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: A Criteria
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Administrator shall develop and publish criteria for the consideration and approval of applications by nonprofit organizations under this subsection.

children.heading: Contents
children.label: B Contents
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, the conditions for participation in the grant program under this subsection shall be the same as the conditions for participation in the program under subsection (), as in effect on .lMay 25, 2007

children.heading: Notification
children.label: C Notification
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of the deadline to submit applications for each fiscal year, the Administrator shall approve or deny any application under this subsection and notify the applicant for each such application.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Administrator shall make a grant for the Federal share of the cost of activities described in the application to each applicant approved under this subsection.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: A grant under this subsection shall be for not more than $150,000, for each year of that grant.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: C Federal share
children.text: The Federal share under this subsection shall be not more than 50 percent.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: D Priority
children.text: In allocating funds made available for grants under this section, the Administrator shall give applications under this subsection or subsection () priority over first-time applications under subsection (b).l

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator may renew a grant under this subsection for additional 3-year periods, if the nonprofit organization submits an application for such renewal at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Administrator may establish.

children.heading: Unlimited renewals
children.label: B Unlimited renewals
children.text: There shall be no limitation on the number of times a grant may be renewed under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: the Administrator is ordered to make such a disclosure by a court in any civil or criminal enforcement action initiated by a Federal or State agency; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Administrator considers such a disclosure to be necessary for the purpose of conducting a financial audit of a women’s business center, but a disclosure under this subparagraph shall be limited to the information necessary for such audit.

children.label: A
children.text: restrict Administration access to program activity data; or

children.label: B
children.text: prevent the Administration from using client information (other than the information described in subparagraph (A)) to conduct client surveys.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Administrator shall issue regulations to establish standards for requiring disclosures during a financial audit under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Study and report on representation of women
children.label: o Study and report on representation of women

children.heading: Study
children.label: 1 Study
children.text: The Administrator shall periodically conduct a study to identify industries, as defined under the North American Industry Classification System, underrepresented by small business concerns owned and controlled by women.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , and every 5 years thereafter, the Administrator shall submit to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship of the Senate and the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives a report on the results of each study under paragraph (1) conducted during the 5-year period ending on the date of the report.January 2, 2013
